name: empty_contains_zero
code:
proof fn phi_1_empty_contains_zero(cm: CommitMask)
    requires
        cm@ == Set::<int>::empty(),
    ensures
        cm@.contains(0),
{
}
reason: The empty commit mask should not contain element 0; if entailed, the spec contradicts emptiness.

name: empty_contains_boundary_511
code:
proof fn phi_2_empty_contains_boundary_511(cm: CommitMask)
    requires
        cm@ == Set::<int>::empty(),
    ensures
        cm@.contains(511),
{
}
reason: The empty mask should not contain the maximum valid bit index 511; if entailed, the ensures is over-permissive.

name: empty_is_nonempty
code:
proof fn phi_3_empty_is_nonempty(cm: CommitMask)
    requires
        cm@ == Set::<int>::empty(),
    ensures
        exists|i: int| cm@.contains(i),
{
}
reason: An empty set must have no members; if an element exists, the postcondition is inconsistent.

name: empty_equals_singleton
code:
proof fn phi_4_empty_equals_singleton(cm: CommitMask)
    requires
        cm@ == Set::<int>::empty(),
    ensures
        cm@ =~= Set::<int>::empty().insert(0),
{
}
reason: The empty set should never be extensionally equal to a singleton set; if entailed, the spec is unsound.

name: empty_contains_negative
code:
proof fn phi_5_empty_contains_negative(cm: CommitMask)
    requires
        cm@ == Set::<int>::empty(),
    ensures
        cm@.contains(-1),
{
}
reason: Negative indices are outside the valid bit range; if the empty mask contains −1, the view abstraction is broken.
